extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won the 26.1km ITT by 31 seconds over van Garderen, taking his first WorldTour victory while YATES Adam inherited race lead by 4 seconds from Tunes.
tactical_patterns: Van Aert beat Dumoulin by 47 seconds despite being cyclocross specialist, suggesting TT form peaks unexpectedly early season for multi-discipline riders.
Yates took race lead by exactly 4 seconds over Tunes, indicating GC contenders barely matched specialist TT riders like van Garderen.
Jumbo-Visma placed two riders in top four (van Aert 1st, Krijswijk 4th) despite lacking traditional TT specialist, revealing depth in discipline.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Beat Dumoulin by 47 seconds and van Garderen by 31 seconds for first WorldTour win, signaling elite TT ability beyond cyclocross reputation.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Only lost 56 seconds to van Aert over 26.1km, strong descending mentioned, competitive TT ability unexpected for pure climber.
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: Only third at 47 seconds down to van Aert as former world TT champion, concerning form indicator before Tour.
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: Lost 1:20 to van Aert including 30 seconds to Buchmann alone, chronic TT weakness confirmed for GC ambitions.
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: positive: Second at 31 seconds, described as 'rock-solid' and 'eating up the road', best of traditional GC contenders.
rider_signals.TUNES Dylan: negative: Lost yellow jersey by exactly 4 seconds after being 'talked about' pre-stage, failed to deliver on TT expectations.
